collection.title: Population dynamics of migratory songbirds in temperate forests
collection.authors: Chen, L.; Morales, R.; Dubois, P.
collection.abstract: We tracked 14 species of neotropical migratory songbirds across three breeding seasons using automated radio telemetry arrays. Our data reveal significant shifts in arrival phenology correlated with spring temperature anomalies. We model population-level consequences of phenological mismatch with peak arthropod availability.

collection.title: Crystal structure of human ACE2 bound to a computationally designed miniprotein inhibitor
collection.authors: Nakamura, T.; Fischer, K.; Okonkwo, E.
collection.abstract: We report the 1.8 Å crystal structure of human ACE2 in complex with a de novo designed 56-residue miniprotein that blocks SARS-CoV-2 spike binding. The miniprotein achieves sub-nanomolar affinity through an optimised helical interface. These structural data inform next-generation inhaled antivirals.

collection.title: Directed evolution of a broadly neutralising antibody against influenza B neuraminidase
collection.authors: Patel, S.; Yamamoto, H.; Garcia, M.
collection.abstract: Using a yeast surface display platform, we evolved a human monoclonal antibody to bind conserved epitopes on influenza B neuraminidase across both Victoria and Yamagata lineages. We characterised escape mutations and mapped residues critical for immune evasion.

collection.title: A simplified reverse genetics system for generating recombinant H5N1 influenza viruses
collection.authors: Kim, D.; Volkov, A.; Petersen, N.
collection.author_corresponding_institution: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology
collection.abstract: We present a streamlined eight-plasmid reverse genetics system for generating recombinant H5N1 avian influenza viruses in standard BSL-3 facilities. Our approach reduces the cloning steps from twelve to four and achieves 10-fold higher rescue efficiency than existing protocols. We demonstrate the system by introducing targeted mutations in the HA and PB2 genes.

collection.title: Single-cell RNA sequencing of murine intestinal epithelium reveals novel stem cell markers
collection.authors: O'Brien, K.; Johansson, S.; Li, W.
collection.abstract: We performed single-cell RNA sequencing on 45,000 cells from murine small intestinal crypts, identifying twelve transcriptionally distinct populations. We describe three previously uncharacterised stem cell subsets defined by co-expression of Lgr5 and novel surface markers amenable to FACS isolation.

collection.title: De novo design of a protein toxin inhibitor using deep learning-guided backbone generation
collection.authors: Zheng, Y.; Adeyemi, F.; Larsson, M.
collection.author_corresponding_institution: Institute for Protein Design, University of Washington
collection.abstract: We used RFdiffusion to design de novo protein binders targeting the receptor-binding domain of Clostridium botulinum neurotoxin serotype A. The top designs achieved picomolar binding affinity and neutralised toxin activity in cell-based assays. We provide the complete computational pipeline and all protein sequences.

collection.title: Metagenomic surveillance of novel bat coronaviruses in Southeast Asian cave systems
collection.authors: Tran, V.; Suryadi, B.; Martinez, C.
collection.author_corresponding_institution: Pasteur Institute Ho Chi Minh City
collection.abstract: We conducted longitudinal metagenomic surveillance of horseshoe bat colonies across 23 cave sites in Vietnam and Indonesia over 18 months. We identified 14 novel sarbecoviruses, three of which utilise human ACE2 for cell entry in pseudovirus assays. Full genome sequences and receptor binding characterisation data are provided.

collection.title: Improved methods for large-scale CRISPR screens in primary human T cells
collection.authors: Anderson, R.; Mbeki, T.; Fujita, S.
collection.author_corresponding_institution: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
collection.abstract: We describe an optimised lentiviral delivery protocol for genome-wide CRISPR-Cas9 screens in primary human CD4+ and CD8+ T cells. Our approach achieves 85% editing efficiency with minimal toxicity, enabling identification of genes essential for T cell activation, exhaustion, and cytokine production.

collection.title: Bayesian phylogeographic analysis of H5N1 clade 2.3.4.4b spread in North American wild birds
collection.authors: Williams, D.; Park, J.; Osei, A.
collection.abstract: We reconstructed the spatiotemporal dynamics of HPAI H5N1 clade 2.3.4.4b dissemination across North American flyways using 2,400 complete genomes collected between 2021 and 2025. Bayesian phylogeographic models identify the Mississippi flyway as the primary corridor for southward spread, with three independent introductions from Eurasia.

collection.title: A transformer-based model for predicting antimicrobial resistance from whole-genome sequences
collection.authors: Gupta, A.; Nilsson, E.; da Silva, R.
collection.abstract: We present AMRFormer, a transformer architecture trained on 120,000 bacterial whole-genome sequences with paired phenotypic resistance data across 15 antibiotic classes. The model achieves 94.2% accuracy in predicting resistance profiles from raw assemblies and identifies novel candidate resistance determinants in Klebsiella pneumoniae.
